rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RONALD C. BERTOLOZZI
rulings.Rulings: The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Petitioner concedes that she is in privity with the petitioners in the prior action, which necessarily determined that those petitioners did not establish extrinsic fraud (despite having attempted to make such a showing). Res judicata bars the prior petitioners (or those in privity with them) from bringing a second action to present a different factual case to establish extrinsic fraud, and the proposed amended petition offers no facts that would permit the current petitioner, having conceded privity, to escape that bar. Therefore, the current petitioner is no more entitled to a new determination on the validity of the will than were the prior petitioners. Any Party wishing to argue the motion must advise opposing parties and notify the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) by 5:00 p.m. today, July 25, 2012.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE EDDIE GALLOWAY & MARY CATHERINE GALLOWAY LIVING TRUST OF SEPTEMBER 1993
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens, Supporting Declaration Of Denolia Eves And Supporting Memorandum
rulings.Rulings: The Motion to Expunge Lis Pendens is denied. The moving party has not presented evidence demonstrating a failure of required notice. The responding party has presented evidence showing that the petition includes a real property claim with probable validity. Any Party wishing to argue the motion must advise opposing parties and notify the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) by 5:00 p.m. today, July 25, 2012.
